---
title: "Family"
generated: true
description: "An exponential family together with a link, as a record of pure functions."
---

[â€ą back to `nitrix.stats.glm`](../nitrix-stats-glm.qmd)

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats._family`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Family -->
```python
class Family
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:Family -->
An exponential family together with a link, as a record of pure functions.

Frozen and hashable so it rides as a static configuration argument (and a
future `custom_vjp` non-differentiable argument).  The fields are the
GLM/IRLS primitives:

- `link` / `linkinv` -- the link $g(\mu)$ and inverse link
  $g^{-1}(\eta)$.
- `mu_eta` -- the derivative $\mathrm{d}\mu / \mathrm{d}\eta$ as
  a function of $\eta$ (an IRLS weight).
- `variance` -- the variance function $V(\mu)$.
- `unit_deviance` -- the per-observation deviance contribution
  $d(y, \mu)$.
- `init_mu` -- a fitted-mean initialiser from $y$ (the IRLS start).
- `loglik` -- the per-observation log-likelihood
  $\ell(y, \mu, \phi)$ (the dispersion argument $\phi$ is
  ignored by the fixed-dispersion families).
- `has_fixed_dispersion` -- `True` when the dispersion is 1 (binomial /
  Poisson; negative-binomial with a *known* `alpha`); `False` when it is
  estimated (Gaussian, Gamma).
- `eta_bound` -- the IRLS clamp on the linear predictor
  ($\infty$ for the bounded identity / logit links; `_ETA_MAX` for
  the unbounded exponential links, where it stabilises the working
  response).  The IRLS core clamps $\eta$ to
  $\pm$ `eta_bound`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Family.clip_eta -->
```python
clip_eta(eta: Array) -> Array
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Clamp the linear predictor to the family's numerically-sane range.

Clamps $\eta$ to $\pm$ `eta_bound` -- $\infty$
for the bounded identity / logit links (a no-op), `_ETA_MAX` for the
unbounded exponential links (where an un-clamped $\eta$ blows up
$\exp(\eta)$ and the IRLS weights).  This is the single source
of truth for the clamp across the IRLS, PQL, and Laplace
working-response sites.

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Family.with_link -->
```python
with_link(link: Union[str, 'Link']) -> 'Family'
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

A copy of this family with a **non-canonical** link substituted.

Only the link triple (`link` / `linkinv` / `mu_eta`) and the IRLS
`eta_bound` change; the distribution -- `variance` / `unit_deviance`
/ `loglik` / `init_mu` (all functions of $\mu$, not
$\eta$) -- is untouched, so e.g. `BINOMIAL.with_link('probit')`
is the probit model and `POISSON.with_link('sqrt')` the
square-root-link Poisson, exactly as in `statsmodels`'
`Family(link=...)` or `mgcv`.  The shared IRLS core is Fisher scoring
(weight $w = (\mathrm{d}\mu/\mathrm{d}\eta)^2 / V$ and working
response $z = \eta + (y - \mu) / (\mathrm{d}\mu/\mathrm{d}\eta)$),
so any link composes without a solver change.

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Family.__init__ -->
```python
__init__(name: str, has_fixed_dispersion: bool, link: Callable[[Array], Array], linkinv: Callable[[Array], Array], mu_eta: Callable[[Array], Array], variance: Callable[[Array], Array], unit_deviance: Callable[[Array, Array], Array], init_mu: Callable[[Array], Array], loglik: Callable[[Array, Array, Array], Array], eta_bound: float = float('inf')) -> None
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
